rule: I describe how mindX is positioned, not how a competitor falls short. Comparisons are factual ('mindX uses BDI; Bittensor uses subnet emissions') not evaluative ('mindX is better than Bittensor'). I never use a competitor's name as a foil.

bittensor.their_thesis: AI Bitcoin — decentralized neural network training via subnet emissions
bittensor.our_position: PYTHAI runs on every L1; we are not an L1 ourselves. Bittensor mining is one possible substrate that mindX agents can consume.

olas.their_thesis: Olas Proof-of-Active-Agent — agent legitimacy through measured activity
olas.our_position: We borrow the legitimacy framing without copying the emission curve. mindX uses the three-receipt model (Tessera + X402 + MarketingAttributionReceipt) for proof of authority + payment + attribution.

virtuals.their_thesis: Tokenized agent ownership through bonding-curve launches
virtuals.our_position: AgenticPlace is mindX's marketplace; agents register via ENS subnames (\*.bankon.eth) and AgentRegistry (ERC-8004), priced through x402. Virtuals tokenizes agent ownership; we tokenize agent identity.

elizaos.their_thesis: Agent framework with strong character/lore primitives
elizaos.our_position: ElizaOS is a framework; mindX is a Counsellor cabinet on a constitutional substrate. Different layer of the stack.

story_protocol.our_position: Story handles IP; mindX handles cognition. Composable — a mindX agent can register a piece of generated content as a Story IP asset.

ai16z.their_thesis: AI-led DAO with strong founder narrative
ai16z.our_position: ai16z is a DAO that uses agents; mindX is the Counsellor cabinet that any DAO can adopt.

manus.our_position: Manus's invite-code reselling and AI-slop backlash is the failure mode mindX is structurally immune to: every action is signed, attested, and traceable through the three-receipt model.
